title: Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open
summary: Study in Morocco with a Fully Funded PhD Opportunity at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic Are you looking for a fully funded PhD opportunity in 2026? Applications are now open for more than 200 fully funded doctoral positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) in Morocco for the 2026–2027 academic year . This prestigious opportunity is offered through the Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) , UM6P s dedicated doctoral training hub that promotes excellence, innovation, and multidisciplinary research. The university welcomes talented researchers from Morocco, Africa, and around the world who are eager to contribute to solving some of the most pressing global and regional challenges. About the Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) The Center for Doctoral Studies (CEDoc) at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic is committed to developing the next generation of researchers, innovators, and thought leaders. CEDoc provides doctoral candidates with: High-quality academic supervision Access to world-class research facilities Interdisciplinary research opportunities Collaboration across multiple research centers and institutes Mentorship from internationally recognized experts An innovation-driven research environment The program is designed to equip PhD candidates with the skills, knowledge, and experience needed to excel in academia, industry, policy, and entrepreneurship. Fully Funded PhD Research Areas Available The 2026–2027 PhD call includes opportunities across numerous research units and disciplines. Africa Business School (ABS) Research topics include: Dynamic Pricing under Uncertainty and Market Power Designing Better Organizations through Behavioral Science, Collective Intelligence, and Artificial Intelligence Digital Technologies and Women Entrepreneurship in Morocco Climate Impacts on Household Finance Artificial Intelligence and Economic Power: Utopia or Dystopia? Africa Center of Manufacturing Excellence (ACME) Research topics include: Multi-Modal NDT Data Fusion for Structural Integrity Assessment Additive Manufacturing of Structured Catalysts and Sorbents Advanced Lightweight Sandwich Structures for Aerospace Applications AI-Based Multi-Sensor Inspection for Composite Structures Explainable Digital Twins for Predictive Maintenance College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES) Research topics include: Hydroclimatic Shifts in North Africa Groundwater and Water Resource Management Water Cycle Dynamics in Semi-Arid Regions Climate Change Adaptation Research Sustainable Agriculture and Environmental Systems Additional opportunities are available across several other UM6P research institutes and centers, covering fields such as engineering, data science, artificial intelligence, sustainability, economics, manufacturing, environmental sciences, and emerging technologies. Who Can Apply? Applicants should hold: A Master s degree An Engineering degree Or an equivalent qualification relevant to the proposed research area Candidates from all nationalities are encouraged to apply. Application Deadline 📅 Application Deadline: July 15, 2026 Interested applicants should review the available research topics carefully before submitting their applications. Important: Candidates may apply to a maximum of two PhD subjects . Why Choose UM6P? University Mohammed VI Polytechnic has emerged as one of Africa s leading research universities, with a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, entrepreneurship, and impact-driven research. Benefits of joining UM6P include: Access to cutting-edge research infrastructure Collaboration with leading international researchers Strong industry and institutional partnerships A vibrant international academic community Opportunities to contribute to Africa s development and global scientific advancement How to Apply Prospective applicants can explore the available doctoral research topics and submit their applications through the official UM6P doctoral admissions portal. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these positions. Final Thoughts For aspiring researchers seeking a fully funded PhD in Africa, the University Mohammed VI Polytechnic s 2026–2027 doctoral recruitment campaign presents an exceptional opportunity. With over 200 funded positions spanning diverse disciplines, world-class research facilities, and a commitment to innovation and excellence, UM6P offers an ideal environment to pursue advanced research and make a meaningful impact. Don t miss your chance to join one of Africa s fastest-growing research institutions and advance your academic and professional career. Key Information at a Glance Institution: University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) Program: Fully Funded PhD Positions Number of Positions: 200+ Academic Year: 2026–2027 Location: Morocco Eligible Applicants: Master s degree, Engineering degree, or equivalent holders Application Limit: Maximum of 2 research subjects Deadline: July 15, 2026 For questions regarding the application process, applicants may contact: cedoc@um6p.ma How to Apply Prospective applicants can explore the available doctoral research topics and submit their applications through the official UM6P doctoral admissions portal. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of these positions. The post Over 200 Fully Funded PhD Positions at University Mohammed VI Polytechnic (UM6P) for 2026–2027: Applications Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions
summary: Applications Open for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme FirstBank has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, offering ambitious young graduates across Ghana and Nigeria an exciting opportunity to begin a rewarding career in banking and financial services. As one of Africa s most respected and established financial institutions, FirstBank continues to invest in developing the next generation of banking professionals through its flagship Graduate Trainee Programme. This strategic initiative is specifically designed to attract, develop, and retain exceptional graduates who will contribute to the bank s long-term growth, innovation, and transformation agenda across Africa. The programme provides participants with an opportunity to gain practical industry experience, develop leadership competencies, build professional networks, and acquire the skills necessary to thrive in the rapidly evolving financial services sector. About the FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme The Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme serves as a talent development platform aimed at identifying high-potential graduates and equipping them with the technical, professional, and leadership skills required to excel within the banking industry. The programme reflects FirstBank s commitment to nurturing young African talent and creating a pipeline of future leaders capable of driving innovation, operational excellence, and sustainable growth across the organization s Pan-African operations. Selected candidates will have the opportunity to work within a dynamic and professional environment while receiving structured training and exposure to various aspects of banking operations. Why You Should Apply The FirstBank Graduate Trainee Programme offers numerous benefits for aspiring banking professionals. Key Benefits Professional Career Development Participants gain access to structured training programs designed to accelerate professional growth and career advancement. Industry Experience Successful candidates will acquire hands-on experience within one of Africa s largest and most respected banking institutions. Exposure to Banking Operations Trainees gain valuable insights into banking operations, customer relationship management, financial services, risk management, digital banking, and business development. Networking Opportunities Participants will have opportunities to connect with experienced professionals, industry leaders, mentors, and fellow trainees across the organization. Leadership Development The programme is designed to develop future leaders who can contribute to the bank s long-term strategic objectives. Long-Term Career Opportunities Outstanding trainees may position themselves for long-term employment opportunities and career progression within FirstBank s Pan-African network. Competencies and Attributes Required FirstBank is seeking highly motivated graduates who demonstrate strong professional and personal competencies. Applicants should possess: Excellent Communication Skills Strong verbal and written communication skills are essential for effective collaboration and customer engagement. Strong Analytical Skills Candidates should be capable of analyzing information, identifying trends, and supporting business decision-making processes. Interpersonal and Relationship Management Skills The ability to build productive relationships with customers, colleagues, and stakeholders is highly valued. Self-Motivation and Results Orientation Applicants should demonstrate initiative, accountability, and a strong drive to achieve results. Innovation and Problem-Solving Ability The bank seeks graduates who can think creatively, identify opportunities, and develop practical solutions to challenges. Teamwork and Collaboration Successful candidates should be effective team players capable of working in diverse and dynamic environments. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must not be older than 28 years as of 30 June 2026. Educational Qualification Candidates must possess: A Bachelor s Degree with a minimum of Second Class Lower division; or An HND qualification with a minimum of Upper Credit. National Service Requirement Applicants must have completed their National Service Scheme (NSS) on or before 30 June 2026. What FirstBank Is Looking For The programme is designed to identify individuals who have the potential to become future leaders within the banking sector. The ideal candidate should demonstrate: Strong academic performance Leadership potential Professional integrity Adaptability and resilience Customer-focused thinking Strong work ethic Passion for banking and financial services Ability to learn quickly and embrace change Programme Locations The 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme is currently accepting applications from candidates in: Ghana Nigeria Similar programmes are also available across other African markets within the FirstBank network. Application Deadline Interested applicants must submit their applications before: 28 June 2026 Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. How to Apply Eligible candidates should submit their applications through the official FirstBank careers portal. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure that all required information is accurately completed before submission. Apply Here Final Thoughts The 2026 FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme presents an exceptional opportunity for talented graduates seeking to launch a successful career within Africa s banking and financial services sector. With access to structured learning, professional mentorship, practical experience, and exposure to one of the continent s leading financial institutions, this programme can serve as a powerful foundation for long-term career success. If you meet the eligibility requirements and aspire to build a meaningful career in banking, now is the time to apply and take the first step toward becoming part of FirstBank s next generation of leaders. For more information on this internship visit here For more internship opportunities on OFY visit here The post FirstBank Pan-African Graduate Trainee Programme 2026 for Young Graduates in Ghana and Nigeria | Launch Your Banking Career with One of Africa’s Leading Financial Institutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities
summary: The African Centre for Cities (ACC) at the University of Cape Town (UCT) has announced an exciting opportunity for a full-time Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) to join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) Data Programme. This position offers professionals passionate about urban development, city governance, leadership, research, and programme management the opportunity to contribute to one of Africa s most innovative city leadership initiatives. The successful candidate will play a critical role in coordinating the City Hall Fellowship Programme while simultaneously providing administrative and operational support to the AMALI Data Programme. The position is offered on a 12-month contract from approximately June 2026 to June 2027, with the possibility of renewal. Applications close on 26 June 2026 . About the African Centre for Cities (ACC) Established in 2007, the African Centre for Cities is a leading urban research institute based at the University of Cape Town. The centre is housed within the School of Architecture, Planning and Geomatics in the Faculty of Engineering and the Built Environment. ACC is recognized across Africa and internationally for its interdisciplinary approach to urban research, policy development, city leadership, and sustainable urban transformation. Drawing expertise from across the University of Cape Town, the centre addresses some of the most pressing urban challenges facing African cities today. The institution works across several thematic areas including: Urban Governance, Politics and the State Urban Health Urban Food Systems Sustainable Infrastructure Transitions Inclusive and Circular Economies Culture, Technology and Society Teaching and Learning the City Other Urban Development Themes About the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) The African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) is a flagship programme housed within the African Centre for Cities. The initiative focuses on supporting African mayors and city leaders in achieving their long-term development goals and creating lasting legacies within their cities. AMALI provides mayors with ongoing technical assistance, leadership support, skills development, and strategic advisory services throughout the course of a calendar year. A major component of the programme is the AMALI Data Programme, which supports city executives and municipal data teams in: Collecting high-quality city-level data Conducting data analysis Using evidence-based decision-making tools Improving city-scale reporting systems Strengthening urban governance through data-driven leadership The AMALI Data Programme currently consists of: A Data Programme Lead Four City Data Advisors The Project Coordinator will become an integral member of this growing and impactful team. About the City Hall Fellowship Programme One of the most exciting aspects of this position is the opportunity to oversee the City Hall Fellowship Programme. Through this initiative, Master s and PhD students are embedded in participating AMALI cities across Africa for up to ten weeks. These placements provide students with practical experience while helping city leaders address key urban challenges and advance their legacy projects. The fellowship programme creates a bridge between academic research and real-world city governance by placing talented postgraduate students directly within municipal environments. The Project Coordinator will ensure that these placements are strategically designed, effectively managed, and deliver meaningful outcomes for both students and participating cities. Key Responsibilities of the Project Coordinator 1. Coordinating the City Hall Fellowship Programme The Project Coordinator will oversee all aspects of the fellowship programme. Responsibilities include: Establishing and Managing Advisory Structures Set up an advisory group consisting of conveners from relevant Master s programmes at UCT. Coordinate regular advisory meetings. Facilitate expert guidance on programme development and implementation. Programme Alignment and Stakeholder Engagement Collaborate closely with AMALI team members and external partners. Ensure the fellowship programme supports the broader objectives of the AMALI initiative. Strengthen partnerships with participating cities and academic institutions. Student Recruitment and Selection Design and manage recruitment processes. Advertise fellowship opportunities. Coordinate application reviews. Establish selection committees where necessary. Support transparent and competitive selection processes. Placement Coordination Engage with participating AMALI cities to identify placement opportunities. Match students with appropriate city placements. Connect students with suitable advisors and mentors. Facilitate productive relationships between fellows and city stakeholders. Logistics and Operational Management Coordinate travel arrangements. Organize accommodation and placement logistics. Ensure students are effectively integrated into host city environments. Manage administrative requirements throughout the fellowship period. Monitoring and Quality Assurance Support supervisors and mentors throughout student placements. Monitor fellowship progress. Ensure fellows produce high-quality outputs that directly support city leaders legacy goals. Facilitate continuous improvement of programme delivery. Documentation and Reporting Maintain comprehensive programme records. Document successes, lessons learned, and programme outcomes. Promote the fellowship programme through communication and outreach activities. Prepare reports for ACC leadership and programme funders. Administrative Support for the AMALI Data Programme The second major responsibility of the role involves supporting the operational effectiveness of the AMALI Data Programme. The successful candidate will help ensure that programme milestones are achieved efficiently and on schedule. Responsibilities include: Programme Administration Provide administrative support to the Data Programme Lead. Support City Data Advisors in their activities. Maintain effective programme coordination systems. Assist with scheduling and programme management. Travel Coordination Organize domestic and international travel logistics. Coordinate meetings and events. Manage travel-related documentation and arrangements. Office Management Support the management of the AMALI Data Programme office. Ensure operational systems run efficiently. Assist in maintaining a productive working environment. Why This Opportunity Matters The Project Coordinator role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to urban transformation across Africa. Successful candidates will: Work with influential African mayors and city leaders. Help shape data-driven governance across African cities. Support future urban leaders through postgraduate fellowships. Engage with international urban development networks. Gain experience within one of Africa s leading urban research institutions. Contribute to evidence-based policy and city development initiatives. Working Environment The role is based within the University of Cape Town, one of Africa s highest-ranked universities and a globally respected research institution. The African Centre for Cities provides an intellectually stimulating and collaborative environment that combines academic excellence with practical impact. Professionals joining the team will work at the intersection of: Research Urban governance Public policy Leadership development Data-driven decision-making Capacity building Employment Equity and Diversity The University of Cape Town is committed to excellence, diversity, inclusion, and transformation. As a designated employer, UCT seeks to achieve its Employment Equity goals and targets and will give preference to candidates from under-represented designated groups in accordance with its Employment Equity Plan. The university actively promotes equal opportunity and encourages applications from diverse backgrounds. Application Deadline Applications for the Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) position close on: 26 June 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to review the official job advert and submit their applications before the closing date. Apply Here Final Thoughts The Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) vacancy at the African Centre for Cities represents an exceptional opportunity for professionals passionate about urban transformation, city leadership, programme management, and higher education engagement. By coordinating fellowship placements across African cities and supporting the AMALI Data Programme, the successful candidate will play a direct role in strengthening city governance, advancing data-driven decision-making, and empowering the next generation of urban leaders across the continent. For professionals seeking a meaningful career at the intersection of urban development, research, leadership, and public impact, this role offers an outstanding platform to contribute to Africa s urban future. For more information on this job opportunity visit here For more job opportunities on OFY click here The post University of Cape Town Project Coordinator (City Hall Fellowship) Vacancy 2026: Join the African Mayoral Leadership Initiative and Shape the Future of African Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 100+ Accelerator 2026: Global Startup Program Offering Funding, Mentorship, Pilot Opportunities, and Corporate Partnerships for Sustainability Innovators
summary: Applications Open for the 100+ Accelerator: Scale Your Sustainability Startup with Global Industry Leaders Applications are now open for the 100+ Accelerator, one of the world s leading sustainability-focused startup accelerator programs designed to help innovators develop, pilot, and scale transformative solutions that address some of the most pressing environmental and social challenges facing the world today. The 100+ Accelerator brings together groundbreaking startups, global corporations, investors, and sustainability leaders to accelerate innovation that can create meaningful impact across industries and supply chains. Through funding, mentorship, pilot opportunities, and strategic partnerships, the program empowers entrepreneurs to transform bold ideas into scalable solutions with global reach. If your startup is solving critical sustainability challenges and is ready to test, validate, and scale its innovation with the support of major corporate partners, the 100+ Accelerator offers an exceptional opportunity to take your business to the next level. About the 100+ Accelerator The 100+ Accelerator was established with a simple but ambitious mission: to fuel innovation and empower startups capable of solving some of the world s biggest sustainability challenges. The program believes that meaningful change happens when entrepreneurs, corporations, investors, and ecosystem partners work together to develop practical solutions that create long-term environmental and social impact. Through collaboration and innovation, the accelerator helps startups transform promising ideas into scalable businesses capable of delivering measurable results across global markets. The program s philosophy is built around three key principles: 1. Fueling Innovation Supporting entrepreneurs who are developing breakthrough technologies and business models that address critical sustainability issues. 2. Empowering Startups Providing resources, mentorship, funding opportunities, and strategic guidance needed for startup growth. 3. Scaling Impact Helping successful innovations move beyond pilot stages to create meaningful impact at scale across industries and global supply chains. Why the 100+ Accelerator Matters Organizations worldwide are increasingly seeking innovative solutions to address challenges related to: Climate change Resource scarcity Sustainable supply chains Water management Waste reduction Circular economy development Renewable energy adoption Sustainable agriculture Carbon emissions reduction Responsible manufacturing Startups often possess the creativity and agility necessary to solve these challenges but may lack the resources, partnerships, and market access needed to scale their innovations. The 100+ Accelerator bridges this gap by connecting startups directly with major corporations and industry leaders capable of supporting commercialization and growth. Program Benefits Participating startups gain access to numerous resources designed to accelerate growth and impact. Funding Opportunities Selected startups receive support to develop and implement pilot projects that demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of their solutions. Corporate Partnerships Participants collaborate with major multinational companies that can provide: Industry expertise Technical guidance Market access Pilot opportunities Strategic partnerships Mentorship and Coaching Founders receive guidance from: Corporate leaders Industry experts Investors Entrepreneurs Sustainability specialists Pilot Project Development The accelerator enables startups to test and validate their solutions in real-world operational environments. Global Visibility Selected startups gain exposure to: Investors Industry leaders Policymakers Media outlets Sustainability networks Access to a Global Innovation Community Participants become part of an international ecosystem of entrepreneurs, experts, and organizations working to build a more sustainable future. Cohort 7: Building the Future of Sustainability The accelerator recently welcomed Cohort 7, bringing together innovative founders, corporate partners, and sustainability leaders from around the world. The cohort represents a diverse group of startups working to create scalable solutions that improve sustainability across global supply chains and industries. Through collaboration, mentorship, and pilot implementation, Cohort 7 participants are helping shape a more resilient and sustainable future. Demo Day Success The program s impact continues to grow each year. At Demo Day 2025, the accelerator brought together: More than 470 founders Corporate partners Investors Sustainability professionals Industry experts The event featured: Startup pitches Industry discussions Innovation showcases Partnership opportunities Networking sessions Demo Day demonstrated the power of collaboration in accelerating sustainability innovation and scaling impactful solutions. What Types of Startups Should Apply? The 100+ Accelerator is ideal for startups working on innovations that address sustainability challenges in areas such as: Climate and Environmental Solutions Carbon reduction technologies Climate adaptation solutions Renewable energy innovations Environmental monitoring technologies Circular Economy Waste reduction systems Recycling innovations Resource efficiency technologies Sustainable materials Water Stewardship Water conservation technologies Water quality solutions Smart water management systems Sustainable Agriculture Agricultural technology Regenerative farming solutions Food system innovations Supply chain optimization Sustainable Manufacturing Resource-efficient production Green industrial processes Sustainable packaging Supply Chain Innovation Traceability systems Logistics optimization Sustainable sourcing technologies Global Network and Strategic Partners The 100+ Accelerator collaborates with a wide network of globally recognized organizations and innovation leaders. Notable ecosystem partners and collaborators include: World Economic Forum Forbes TED EY Business Insider Fast Company The Aspen Institute One Young World Female Founders Highline Beta Leaders Quest These relationships provide startups with valuable visibility and opportunities to engage with influential stakeholders. Why Startups Should Consider Applying Participating in the 100+ Accelerator can help startups: Validate their technology in real-world environments. Gain access to corporate customers. Build strategic partnerships. Increase investor readiness. Expand market opportunities. Accelerate product development. Strengthen business models. Scale impact more rapidly. How to Apply Applications are currently open. Interested startups should review the eligibility criteria and application requirements through the official 100+ Accelerator website. Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong submissions that clearly demonstrate: The sustainability challenge being addressed The uniqueness of the solution Potential for scalability Measurable impact Commercial viability Alignment with sustainability goals Apply Here Final Thoughts The 100+ Accelerator continues to be one of the most influential startup programs focused on sustainability innovation worldwide. By connecting entrepreneurs with funding, mentorship, corporate partners, and pilot opportunities, the program helps transform bold ideas into scalable solutions capable of creating lasting environmental and social impact. For startups committed to solving critical sustainability challenges and building a more resilient future, the 100+ Accelerator represents an unparalleled opportunity to accelerate growth, validate solutions, and expand impact on a global scale. For more information on this accelerator opportunity visit here For more global opportunities on OFY, visit here The post 100+ Accelerator 2026: Global Startup Program Offering Funding, Mentorship, Pilot Opportunities, and Corporate Partnerships for Sustainability Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges
summary: Applications Open for the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026 Innovators, researchers, organizations, entrepreneurs, policymakers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to apply for the prestigious GCSP Prize for Innovation in Global Security 2026. Organized by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP), this internationally recognized award celebrates bold, creative, and impactful solutions that address some of the world s most pressing security challenges. Since its launch in 2015, the GCSP Prize for Innovation has become a leading platform for recognizing outstanding achievements in global security. The award honors individuals and organizations that develop innovative approaches, technologies, research, programs, and initiatives capable of improving peace, stability, resilience, and security worldwide. The competition is open to applicants from all sectors and disciplines, reflecting the growing need for interdisciplinary collaboration in addressing modern security threats. About the Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) The Geneva Centre for Security Policy is an international foundation dedicated to advancing peace, security, and international cooperation. Through its educational programs, policy dialogue initiatives, research activities, and innovation-focused projects, the GCSP serves a global community of professionals, institutions, and leaders working to build a more secure world. The Prize for Innovation in Global Security is part of the GCSP s Creativity and Innovation Initiative and is managed by the organization s Geopolitics and Global Futures Department. What Is the GCSP Prize for Innovation? The GCSP Prize for Innovation recognizes exceptional achievements that contribute innovative solutions to contemporary and emerging security challenges. The award seeks to identify transformative ideas and initiatives that have the potential to make a significant impact on global peace and security. Winning projects often demonstrate originality, scalability, effectiveness, and practical relevance to today s complex security environment. The competition welcomes a broad range of innovations, including: New technologies addressing security challenges Research publications advancing security knowledge Policy innovations and governance solutions Conflict prevention initiatives Peacebuilding programs Cybersecurity innovations Humanitarian security interventions Community resilience projects Climate-security solutions Artificial intelligence applications for security Educational initiatives promoting peace and stability Institutional reforms and innovative organizational approaches Prize Benefits Successful applicants will receive international recognition and valuable opportunities to showcase their work to a global audience. First Place Winner The first-place laureate will receive: CHF 10,000 cash prize Certificate of Excellence International recognition from a leading global security institution Increased visibility among policymakers, researchers, practitioners, and security experts Second and Third Place Winners Recipients will receive: Official Certificates of Recognition International exposure and professional recognition Honourable Mentions Two additional projects will receive: Honourable Mention Awards Recognition for outstanding contributions to global security innovation Why You Should Apply Participating in the GCSP Prize for Innovation offers numerous advantages: 1. Global Visibility The award provides an international platform to showcase innovative work before experts, practitioners, governments, international organizations, and global decision-makers. 2. Financial Support The CHF 10,000 cash award can help scale impactful projects and support future innovation efforts. 3. Professional Recognition Winning or being shortlisted by the Geneva Centre for Security Policy can significantly strengthen professional credibility and organizational reputation. 4. Networking Opportunities Participants gain exposure to a global community of innovators, researchers, policymakers, and security professionals. 5. Impact Amplification The competition helps promising solutions reach wider audiences and potential partners capable of expanding their impact. Who Should Apply? The GCSP Prize welcomes applications from a diverse range of actors including: Academic researchers Universities and research institutions Non-governmental organizations Civil society organizations Technology companies Startups Government agencies International organizations Independent innovators Peacebuilders Humanitarian actors Security practitioners Policy experts Social entrepreneurs Applicants from all sectors are encouraged to submit innovative work that demonstrates measurable contributions to global security. Areas of Interest The competition is particularly relevant for projects focused on: International security Peace and conflict resolution Strategic foresight Cybersecurity Artificial intelligence and security Geopolitics Human security Climate and security Countering violent extremism Crisis management Humanitarian innovation Defense innovation Governance and resilience Emerging technologies Global futures and strategic competition Application Deadline Applicants must submit their applications no later than: 23 September 2026 at 23:59 UTC+2 Late applications may not be considered. Winners Announcement The winners of the 2026 GCSP Prize for Innovation will be announced during Fall 2026. How to Apply Interested applicants should review the official rules and regulations and submit their applications through the Geneva Centre for Security Policy application portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their submissions early and ensure that all required information is completed before the deadline. Apply Here Final Thoughts The GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026 represents a remarkable opportunity for individuals and organizations working to transform the future of global security. Whether through technological innovation, groundbreaking research, policy development, or practical peacebuilding initiatives, this award recognizes those whose work is shaping safer and more resilient societies. With a CHF 10,000 cash prize, international recognition, and the opportunity to engage with a global network of security professionals, the GCSP Prize for Innovation remains one of the most prestigious opportunities for innovators committed to advancing peace and security worldwide. If your work is creating meaningful solutions to today s most pressing security challenges, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. For more information on this competition visit here For more global opportunities on OFY, visit here The post GCSP Prize for Innovation 2026: Win CHF 10,000 for Groundbreaking Solutions to Global Security Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD
summary: Aspiring policy professionals, researchers, economists, and international development enthusiasts now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience at one of the world s leading international organizations. The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) Middle East and Africa Division is currently seeking an intern to support the work of the division beginning in September 2026 . This internship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful policy initiatives, regional economic development programmes, and international cooperation efforts focused on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. Candidates with an interest in economic development, investment, competitiveness, entrepreneurship, and public policy are strongly encouraged to apply. While not a mandatory requirement, working knowledge of Arabic is considered a significant advantage . Interested applicants are invited to submit their CV directly to: sara.mouhoud@oecd.org About the OECD The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organization that works with governments, policymakers, and stakeholders worldwide to develop evidence-based policies that promote prosperity, equality, opportunity, and well-being. The OECD serves as a global platform for policy dialogue, research, data analysis, and international cooperation, helping countries address economic, social, and environmental challenges. Working within the OECD provides unparalleled exposure to international policymaking, research, and development initiatives that influence governments and institutions across the globe. About the Middle East and Africa Division The OECD Middle East and Africa Division plays a crucial role in strengthening partnerships and supporting policy reforms across countries in the Middle East and North Africa region. The division works closely with governments, policymakers, private sector stakeholders, development partners, and international institutions to promote: Economic growth Sustainable development Investment promotion Private sector development Entrepreneurship Job creation Economic competitiveness Youth empowerment Women s economic participation Through research, policy dialogue, technical assistance, and capacity-building initiatives, the division contributes to the economic transformation and resilience of countries throughout the region. The MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme One of the flagship initiatives supported by the OECD Middle East and Africa Division is the MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme. Established in 2005 at the request of governments across the Middle East and North Africa, the programme serves as a strategic partnership between OECD and MENA economies. For over two decades, the programme has facilitated: Knowledge exchange Policy dialogue Capacity building Sharing of international best practices Investment promotion initiatives Entrepreneurship development Economic reform support As the programme celebrated its 20th anniversary in 2025, it continues to serve as a critical platform for fostering inclusive, sustainable, and competitive economies throughout the region. Objectives of the MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme The programme operates under the mandate of the 2021 Ministerial Declaration and focuses on supporting reforms that help countries: 1. Mobilize Investment The programme supports efforts to: Improve investment climates Attract foreign direct investment Strengthen investor confidence Enhance regulatory frameworks 2. Strengthen Private Sector Development Activities focus on: Business environment reforms SME development Competitiveness enhancement Innovation promotion 3. Foster Entrepreneurship The programme supports: Startup ecosystems Entrepreneurial capacity building Innovation-driven growth Youth entrepreneurship initiatives 4. Promote Inclusive Growth A major emphasis is placed on creating opportunities for: Young people Women Emerging entrepreneurs Underserved communities Countries Participating in the Programme The MENA-OECD Competitiveness Programme currently engages 18 economies: Algeria Bahrain Djibouti Egypt Iraq Jordan Kuwait Lebanon Libya Mauritania Morocco Oman Palestinian Authority Qatar Saudi Arabia Tunisia United Arab Emirates Yemen These countries collaborate with OECD member states to exchange expertise and develop policies that support economic transformation and sustainable development. How the Programme Operates The programme functions through a structured governance framework that includes: High-Level Ministerial Meetings Senior government officials and policymakers gather to: Set strategic priorities Discuss regional challenges Share policy experiences Coordinate reform efforts Annual Steering Group Meetings The Steering Group monitors: Programme implementation Progress toward objectives Future priorities Regional cooperation initiatives Regional Policy Dialogues The programme organizes policy discussions across multiple thematic work streams to encourage knowledge sharing and collaborative problem-solving. Country-Specific Projects Tailored support is provided through: Dedicated Country Programmes Bilateral initiatives Technical assistance projects Capacity-building activities Notable examples include: OECD Country Programme with Egypt Bilateral initiatives with Morocco Bilateral initiatives with Saudi Arabia What the OECD Internship Could Involve Although specific responsibilities have not been publicly detailed, interns in OECD divisions commonly contribute to: Research and Analysis Conducting background research Reviewing policy developments Analyzing economic and social data Supporting report preparation Programme Support Assisting with project coordination Supporting event organization Preparing briefing materials Contributing to programme activities Stakeholder Engagement Supporting communication with partners Assisting with outreach activities Coordinating meetings and workshops Knowledge Management Compiling research findings Drafting summaries and reports Supporting publication development Benefits of the Internship An internship with the OECD offers numerous professional advantages. International Experience Gain firsthand experience working within a globally respected international organization. Policy Exposure Develop an understanding of: Economic policymaking International cooperation Development strategies Regional reform initiatives Professional Networking Interact with: OECD experts Government officials International development practitioners Researchers Policy specialists Career Development The experience can strengthen future career prospects in: International organizations Government ministries Development agencies Think tanks Research institutions Consulting firms Multilateral organizations Why Arabic Language Skills Are Valuable The Middle East and Africa Division works extensively with stakeholders across the MENA region. As a result, candidates with working knowledge of Arabic may benefit from: Enhanced communication with regional partners Greater understanding of regional contexts Ability to engage with Arabic-language materials Increased effectiveness in programme activities However, Arabic remains an asset rather than a mandatory requirement. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Why You Should Apply An internship with the OECD Middle East and Africa Division provides a rare opportunity to contribute to meaningful policy work that supports economic transformation, entrepreneurship, investment, and sustainable development across the MENA region. For students and young professionals seeking a career in international affairs, economics, public policy, or development, this internship offers valuable exposure to global policymaking processes and the chance to work alongside leading experts shaping the future of economic cooperation and reform. Candidates interested in launching an international policy career are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Click here for more global internship opportunities The post OECD Middle East and Africa Division Internship 2026: Launch Your International Policy Career with the OECD appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France: Applications Open for Students, Young Professionals, and Researchers Driving the Energy Transition
summary: The European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 is now accepting applications for its highly anticipated fifth edition, offering an exceptional opportunity for students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders to engage directly in shaping the future of Europe s energy transition. Scheduled to take place from 26–29 October 2026 in Nice, France, the forum will bring together some of the brightest young minds from across Europe and the Mediterranean region to exchange ideas, collaborate on solutions, strengthen professional networks, and contribute to discussions that will influence the future of sustainable energy systems. Organized by the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN), EYEF has become one of Europe s leading youth platforms dedicated to empowering the next generation of energy professionals and ensuring that youth perspectives are represented in the global energy dialogue. Applications are open from 11 May to 21 June 2026, and selected participants will embark on a multi-month journey that combines virtual learning, collaboration, and networking activities before culminating in the in-person forum in Nice. About the European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) The European Youth Energy Forum is more than a conference. It is a youth-driven platform designed to connect ambitious young people who are passionate about energy, climate action, sustainability, innovation, and policy development. Since its inception, EYEF has provided a unique space where participants can: Discuss pressing energy challenges Explore innovative solutions Learn from industry experts Collaborate across disciplines Build international partnerships Develop leadership skills Contribute to Europe s energy transition agenda The forum recognizes that young people are not simply future stakeholders in the energy sector—they are already playing critical roles in shaping sustainable energy systems, influencing climate action policies, and advancing innovation across industries. EYEF 2026 Theme: From Skills to Impact The 2026 edition of the forum is built around the theme: From Skills to Impact: Empowering Young Professionals to Implement the Energy Transition This theme reflects a growing recognition that achieving energy transition goals requires more than technical knowledge alone. It requires: Leadership Collaboration Innovation Practical implementation skills Cross-sector partnerships Youth engagement The forum seeks to equip participants with the tools, networks, and experiences needed to translate knowledge into real-world impact. Why EYEF 2026 Matters Europe is undergoing one of the most significant energy transformations in history. Countries across the continent are investing heavily in: Renewable energy Green hydrogen Energy efficiency Smart grids Sustainable mobility Climate resilience Decarbonization strategies At the same time, governments, industries, and institutions increasingly recognize the importance of involving young people in decision-making processes. EYEF serves as a bridge between youth talent and the institutions driving Europe s energy future. The forum provides participants with opportunities to: Engage with industry experts Learn from policymakers Connect with researchers Collaborate with peers Develop innovative solutions A Unique International Experience One of the defining features of EYEF is its international and interdisciplinary nature. The forum welcomes participants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds, creating a rich environment for knowledge sharing and collaboration. Participants typically represent fields such as: Renewable energy Engineering Environmental sciences Climate policy Sustainable development Economics Energy law Innovation and entrepreneurship Research and academia This diversity allows participants to explore energy challenges from multiple perspectives and develop more comprehensive solutions. Building on Collaboration with IRENA The European Youth Energy Forum continues its collaboration with the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) Youth Forum. This partnership strengthens connections between regional and global youth energy initiatives and provides participants with greater exposure to international energy discussions. The collaboration contributes to: Global knowledge exchange International networking opportunities Exposure to renewable energy developments Youth participation in global energy governance What Selected Participants Can Expect Participants selected for EYEF 2026 will participate in a comprehensive programme that extends beyond the in-person event. Online Learning Journey Before arriving in Nice, participants will engage in an online programme running from July to October 2026. This phase is designed to: Introduce key energy transition topics Facilitate networking Prepare participants for the forum Encourage collaborative learning In-Person Forum in Nice The in-person event will take place from 26–29 October 2026. During the forum, participants can expect: Interactive workshops Expert discussions Collaborative sessions Networking events Team-based activities Youth-led initiatives Professional development opportunities Long-Term Network Access Participants become part of the wider European Youth Energy Network community, providing opportunities for future collaboration and engagement. Who Should Apply? EYEF is designed for motivated individuals who are passionate about creating a sustainable energy future. Ideal applicants include: Students University students studying: Energy Engineering Environmental sciences Climate studies Public policy Economics Sustainability Young Professionals Early-career professionals working in: Energy companies Utilities Government agencies NGOs Climate organizations Research institutions Researchers Researchers exploring topics related to: Renewable energy Climate change Energy systems Sustainability Energy policy Youth Leaders Young changemakers involved in: Advocacy Climate action Community engagement Youth empowerment Timeline for EYEF 2026 The forum follows a structured journey that combines preparation, learning, and collaboration. Applications 11 May – 21 June 2026 Online Programme July – October 2026 Live Event 26–29 October 2026 Location: Nice, France Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the expected high level of competition. About the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN) The forum is organized by the European Youth Energy Network (EYEN), a youth-led organization dedicated to increasing youth participation in energy discussions and supporting young professionals entering the sector. EYEN works to: Empower young professionals Strengthen youth voices Promote innovation Facilitate networking Support energy transition initiatives The organization has become a leading platform for youth engagement in Europe s energy landscape. The EYEF 2026 Organising Team The forum is coordinated by a dedicated group of volunteers and young professionals from across Europe. Key members include: Daria Alekseeva – EYEF Manager Florentina Oldani – Logistics Coordinator Melav Salih – Programme Coordinator Samantha Chinyoka – Programme Officer Javiera Branes – Programme Officer Szymon Hoffmann – Sponsorship Coordinator Together with many other volunteers, they work to ensure the success and impact of the forum. Partnerships and Collaboration EYEF 2026 is supported by institutional and industry partners committed to advancing youth participation in the energy transition. The forum continues its cooperation with IRENA and actively welcomes new partners interested in supporting youth engagement in energy and sustainability. These partnerships help provide participants with access to: Industry expertise Professional networks Learning opportunities Career development pathways Why You Should Apply Participating in EYEF 2026 can help you: Expand your professional network Learn from energy experts Develop leadership skills Gain international exposure Collaborate on innovative solutions Strengthen your career prospects Join a community of energy changemakers Whether you are just beginning your career or already working within the sector, the forum offers valuable opportunities for growth and impact. APPLY HERE Application Deadline Applications Close: 21 June 2026 Interested applicants should submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of selection. Click HERE to learn more about European Youth Energy Forum (EYEF) 2026 in Nice, France. Conclusion The European Youth Energy Forum 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for students, researchers, young professionals, and emerging leaders who want to contribute to Europe s energy transition. Through its unique blend of online learning, international networking, professional development, and collaborative problem-solving, EYEF equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and connections needed to drive meaningful change in the energy sector. Hosted in the beautiful city of Nice, France, and supported by the European Youth Energy Network and international partners, the forum offers a powerful platform for youth engagement and leadership. Young people who are passionate about renewable energy, climate action, sustainability, innovation, and policy development are strongly encouraged to apply before the 21 June 2026 deadline. Discover more global Conference , European and Environmental Development opportunities on OFY. title: 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme for Young Diplomats: Fully Immersive International Relations Training in Austria and Poland
summary: Young diplomats and emerging international relations professionals from the OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation countries now have an exceptional opportunity to gain firsthand experience within one of the world s most influential security and co-operation organizations. The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE), through its External Co-operation Section, has officially launched the call for nominations for the 2026 Study Visit Programme for OSCE Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation Young Diplomats . This prestigious two-week programme will take place from 28 September to 9 October 2026 and will provide selected participants with unique exposure to the OSCE s work, institutions, and decision-making processes. The programme will be hosted across two major European cities— Vienna, Austria , where the OSCE Secretariat is headquartered, and Warsaw, Poland , home to the OSCE Office for Democratic Institutions and Human Rights (ODIHR). With only eight participants selected for the programme, this highly competitive opportunity offers direct engagement with international diplomacy, multilateral co-operation, democratic governance initiatives, human rights institutions, media freedom mechanisms, and regional security frameworks. About the 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme The OSCE Study Visit Programme is the flagship initiative of the OSCE Secretariat s External Co-operation Section designed to strengthen engagement between the OSCE and its Asian and Mediterranean Partners for Co-operation. The programme provides young diplomats and international affairs professionals with a comprehensive understanding of the OSCE s role in promoting: Regional security Conflict prevention Democratic governance Human rights protection Rule of law Media freedom International co-operation Participants will spend two weeks engaging directly with OSCE experts, policymakers, and institutional representatives through a combination of: Briefings Presentations Interactive discussions Institutional visits Networking opportunities Participation in OSCE-related events The programme offers a rare behind-the-scenes look at how one of the world s largest regional security organizations operates. Programme Dates and Locations Programme Period 28 September – 9 October 2026 Host Locations Vienna, Austria Warsaw, Poland The inclusion of Warsaw as part of the 2026 programme represents an important component of the study visit, allowing participants to engage directly with institutions and stakeholders connected to ODIHR in its host country. What Participants Will Learn Selected participants will gain extensive exposure to the OSCE s work and operational structures. Key learning areas include: 1. Understanding the OSCE s Mandate Participants will explore: The history and evolution of the OSCE Regional security challenges Conflict prevention strategies International diplomacy mechanisms Multilateral decision-making processes Co-operative security frameworks 2. Exposure to OSCE Executive Structures Participants will have the opportunity to visit and interact with various OSCE institutions and executive structures, gaining practical insights into their mandates and operations. 3. Human Rights and Democratic Governance Through engagements with specialized OSCE bodies, participants will deepen their understanding of: Human rights promotion Democratic institution building Election observation Rule of law initiatives Good governance practices 4. Freedom of Expression and Media Freedom The programme will also introduce participants to OSCE efforts related to: Media freedom Protection of journalists Freedom of expression Information integrity Democratic public discourse 5. Parliamentary Engagement Participants will learn how parliamentary diplomacy contributes to international co-operation through interactions with representatives and offices connected to the OSCE Parliamentary Assembly. Institutions Participants Will Visit One of the most valuable aspects of the programme is direct exposure to several key OSCE institutions. These include: OSCE Secretariat The central administrative body supporting the OSCE s activities and operations across participating states and partner countries. OSCE Office for Democratic Institutions and Human Rights (ODIHR) Participants will learn about ODIHR s work in: Human rights Election observation Democratic institution development Rule of law promotion OSCE Representative on Freedom of the Media (RFoM) This institution focuses on: Monitoring media freedom Promoting freedom of expression Supporting independent journalism Addressing media-related challenges OSCE Parliamentary Assembly (OSCEPA) Vienna Office Participants will gain insight into the parliamentary dimension of OSCE activities and international diplomacy. Benefits of Participating in the Programme The programme offers numerous professional and academic advantages, including: Professional Development Exposure to international diplomacy Understanding multilateral institutions Enhanced policy knowledge Increased understanding of regional security issues Networking Opportunities Participants will be able to connect with: OSCE officials International experts Policymakers Fellow young diplomats International affairs professionals Career Advancement The programme can significantly strengthen future career prospects in: Foreign affairs ministries International organizations Diplomatic services Public policy institutions Research organizations Multilateral agencies Practical Learning Experience Participants will move beyond theoretical knowledge and gain firsthand experience of how international organizations operate in practice. Eligibility Criteria Delegations nominating candidates must ensure applicants meet all programme requirements. Candidates must: Be under the age of 35. Have completed a first-level degree from an accredited university. Demonstrate professional fluency in English, both written and spoken. Possess prior work experience in international relations, preferably in a multilateral context. Meeting the eligibility requirements does not guarantee selection, as the programme is highly competitive and limited to eight participants. Application Process For Prospective Candidates Interested applicants should: Contact their relevant OSCE Partner for Co-operation delegation. Express interest in being nominated. Complete the required application process through their delegation. Prepare supporting documents. Nominated candidates will be required to submit: A Curriculum Vitae (CV) A motivational statement explaining their interest in the programme For Delegations Delegations are responsible for forwarding nominated candidates to the OSCE Secretariat through the designated submission channels. Important Deadline Delegation Nomination Deadline 17 July 2026 at 23:59 (CEST) Late nominations may not be considered. Prospective candidates are encouraged to contact their respective delegations well in advance of the deadline to allow sufficient time for the nomination process. Why This Opportunity Matters In today s increasingly interconnected world, effective diplomacy requires a deep understanding of multilateral institutions and international co-operation mechanisms. The 2026 OSCE Study Visit Programme offers a rare opportunity for young diplomats and international affairs professionals to engage directly with experts working at the forefront of security, democracy, human rights, and international co-operation. By participating in this programme, selected candidates will gain practical knowledge, expand their professional networks, and strengthen their capacity to contribute meaningfully to international diplomacy and co-operative security efforts within their respective countries and regions. How to Apply Prospective candidates should contact their relevant OSCE Partner for Co-operation delegation to begin the nomination process. Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early and ensure all required documents are ready ahead of the nomination deadline of 17 July 2026 . title: 2027 Karsh Journalism Fellowship: $4,000 Stipend, Fully Funded U.S. Retreats, and Specialized Training for Journalists
summary: Professional journalists and recent journalism graduates seeking to deepen their understanding of Jewish issues and strengthen their reporting expertise now have an exceptional opportunity to apply for the prestigious 2027 Karsh Journalism Fellowship . Recognized as the world s only journalism fellowship dedicated exclusively to specialized training in Jewish topics, the Karsh Journalism Fellowship offers a unique learning experience that combines immersive retreats, expert mentorship, professional networking, and independent reporting opportunities. Selected fellows will participate in three intensive, fully funded retreats in Los Angeles, New York City, and Washington, D.C. , while engaging with leading journalists, scholars, policymakers, business leaders, artists, and innovators. In addition to receiving comprehensive training and mentorship, fellows will receive a $4,000 stipend , with all travel and programme-related expenses fully covered. Applications for the 2027 cohort are currently open and must be submitted by 1 September 2026 . About the Karsh Journalism Fellowship The Karsh Journalism Fellowship was established to improve public understanding of Jewish issues through rigorous, independent, and impactful journalism. As the only fellowship of its kind in the world, the programme provides journalists with opportunities to gain deeper insights into Jewish history, culture, identity, communities, and contemporary issues while maintaining complete editorial independence. The fellowship is firmly grounded in the journalistic values of: Accuracy Independence Transparency Accountability Importantly, the fellowship is explicitly nonpartisan and does not exercise editorial control over the stories fellows choose to pursue. Participants are encouraged to explore topics through their own professional lens and produce meaningful reporting that contributes to informed public discourse. Fellowship Benefits The Karsh Journalism Fellowship offers a comprehensive package designed to support professional growth, learning, and impactful reporting. 1. $4,000 Fellowship Stipend Each selected fellow receives: A $4,000 stipend Financial support for participation throughout the programme 2. Fully Funded Travel All travel expenses associated with the fellowship are covered, including participation in the programme s three in-person retreats. This allows fellows to focus entirely on learning, networking, and reporting without financial barriers. 3. Three Intensive In-Person Retreats Fellows will participate in immersive retreats hosted in three major U.S. cities: Los Angeles, California Participants will engage with experts and thought leaders from media, arts, culture, and business sectors. New York City, New York Fellows will gain exposure to influential journalists, academics, policymakers, and institutions involved in public affairs and media. Washington, D.C. The nation s capital provides opportunities to engage with policymakers, government officials, and experts involved in shaping public policy and international affairs. These retreats serve as the cornerstone of the fellowship experience, providing intensive learning, discussion, and networking opportunities. 4. Access to Distinguished Experts Throughout the fellowship, participants will engage with: Leading journalists Renowned scholars Policy experts Government officials Business innovators Cultural leaders Media professionals These interactions help fellows broaden their perspectives and strengthen their reporting capabilities. 5. Professional Networking Opportunities The programme creates valuable opportunities to build long-term professional relationships with: Fellow journalists Media organizations Academic experts Industry leaders Public policy professionals These connections can provide ongoing collaboration and career development opportunities long after the fellowship concludes. What Fellows Will Learn The fellowship offers specialized exposure to a wide range of topics related to Jewish communities and issues. Participants may explore areas such as: Jewish history Jewish identity Contemporary Jewish life Public policy issues Religion and society Culture and the arts Media representation International affairs Community development Social and political trends The programme encourages critical thinking, nuanced understanding, and high-quality journalism. Fellowship Requirements Successful applicants must commit fully to all fellowship activities. Mandatory Participation Includes: Three In-Person Retreats Fellows are required to attend all programme retreats in: Los Angeles New York City Washington, D.C. Monthly Virtual Sessions Participants must attend: Monthly one-hour Zoom sessions Ongoing discussions and learning opportunities throughout the fellowship period Final Reporting Project To successfully complete the fellowship, each fellow must: Publish or produce an original reported work Focus on a Jewish topic of their choice Work under the guidance and approval of their editor Maintain full editorial independence The fellowship does not dictate story topics and does not interfere with reporting decisions. Who Can Apply? The programme welcomes applications from journalists across diverse backgrounds and media sectors. Eligible applicants include: Early-Career Journalists Journalists in the early stages of their professional careers seeking specialized expertise and professional development. Mid-Career Journalists Experienced reporters interested in expanding their knowledge and producing deeper reporting on Jewish issues. Recent Journalism School Graduates Graduates entering the journalism profession who wish to strengthen their reporting skills and subject matter expertise. Journalists Across All Platforms The fellowship welcomes professionals working in: Print journalism Digital journalism Broadcast media Television Radio Podcasting Multimedia storytelling Investigative reporting Freelance journalism Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a professional journalist or recent journalism school graduate. Be an early-career or mid-career journalist. Be a U.S. citizen or possess valid work authorization allowing employment in the United States throughout the fellowship period. Commit to attending all required programme activities. Participate in monthly Zoom sessions. Complete and publish a fellowship reporting project. Selection Process The programme is highly competitive. Each year, up to 10 fellows are selected from a pool of talented journalists from across the United States. Selection is based on factors such as: Professional experience Reporting potential Commitment to journalistic excellence Interest in exploring Jewish issues Capacity to contribute meaningful public understanding through journalism Why This Fellowship Stands Out The Karsh Journalism Fellowship offers a rare opportunity for journalists to gain specialized expertise while maintaining complete editorial independence. Unlike many reporting fellowships that focus on narrow thematic objectives, the Karsh Fellowship encourages fellows to explore Jewish topics through their own reporting interests and professional judgment. The programme s emphasis on accuracy, transparency, and nonpartisanship makes it particularly valuable for journalists committed to producing fair, balanced, and impactful reporting. Combined with fully funded retreats, expert mentorship, a generous stipend, and access to influential networks, the fellowship represents one of the most distinctive professional development opportunities available to journalists today. Application Deadline Deadline: 1 September 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early and review programme requirements carefully before submitting. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Journalists interested in expanding their expertise, strengthening their reporting skills, and joining a prestigious network of media professionals are encouraged to apply before the deadline.
